abstract: This study explores the integration of Islamic Education values into the Strengthening Pancasila Student Profile and Work Culture Project (P5BK) at SMKN 1 Masbagik. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive approach, data were gathered through in-depth interviews, field observations, and documentation reviews, subsequently analyzed using the Miles and Huberman interactive model. The findings reveal that Islamic values are woven into P5BK through three core thematic pillars: sustainable lifestyles, employability, and local wisdom. The implementation follows a structured four-stage process—planning, execution, evaluation, and identifying supportive factors—which infuses professional vocational training with spiritual principles such as environmental stewardship (*khalifah fil ardh*) and Islamic work ethics. Key advantages of this integrated model include its operational flexibility, the creation of strong synergies between the school and the industrial community, and the provision of authentic learning experiences that heighten students' social and environmental consciousness. Beyond practical applications, this research offers theoretical support for the "Independent Learning" (*Kurikulum Merdeka*) framework, suggesting that a holistic, value-based approach can significantly enhance teacher competence and the overall quality of character development in vocational education.
